title: Swiggy asks for all your data with one tap. DPDP says that consent is invalid.
subtitle: Focus: the onboarding consent experience — the first legal touchpoint where personal-data collection is authorized.
problem: Swiggy's onboarding bundles essential delivery data (phone, address) with optional behavioural data (location, marketing, analytics) behind a single 'Accept All'. DPDP Act, Section 6 requires consent that is free, specific, informed, unconditional and affirmative — bundling is none of these.
solution: Swiggy SARAL: unbundled consent buckets, just-in-time micro-notices, a multilingual plain-language layer, an immutable Consent Ledger, and a children's-data gateway.
decision_ask: Approve resourcing + Phase-1 A/B launch to make consent valid without breaking onboarding.
notes: Open with the regulatory countdown. This is a product-architecture problem, not a legal checkbox. Bundled consent is a ticking time bomb for both trust and law.
